response:
I'll perform cell type deconvolution on your spatial data using RCTD with the provided single-cell reference dataset. This will estimate the proportion of each cell type within each spot.

```python
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load spatial data (slice 0)
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)
ad_spatial = slice_0.adata.copy()

# Verify it's spot-level data
if not slice_0.is_spot_level:
    raise ValueError("Deconvolution requires spot-level data (Visium)")

print(f"Spatial data: {ad_spatial.n_obs} spots × {ad_spatial.n_vars} genes")

# Load reference single-cell data
ad_sc = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/D_stat/Q17/run_0/dlpfc_reference.h5ad')

print(f"Reference data: {ad_sc.n_obs} cells × {ad_sc.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Cell types in reference: {ad_sc.obs['celltype'].value_counts()}")

# Make sure gene/cell names are unique
ad_spatial.var_names_make_unique()
ad_sc.var_names_make_unique()
ad_spatial.obs_names_make_unique()
ad_sc.obs_names_make_unique()

# Check gene overlap
common_genes = set(ad_spatial.var_names) & set(ad_sc.var_names)
print(f"Common genes between spatial and reference: {len(common_genes)}")
```

```
Spatial data: 3611 spots × 18002 genes
Reference data: 35212 cells × 30062 genes
Cell types in reference: celltype
Ex_10_L2_4       8464
Ex_3_L4_5        3629
Oligos_3         2957

Inhib_2_VIP      1750

Inhib_5          1387
Mix_5             974
Inhib_6_SST       970
Inhib_1           962
Mix_2             947
Ex_6_L4_6         901
Ex_2_L5           895
OPCs_1            833
Ex_8_L5_6         822
Inhib_7_PVALB     692
Micro/Macro       542
Astros_3          512
Ex_1_L5_6         351
Inhib_3_SST       344
OPCs_2            324
Ex_4_L_6          320
Inhib_8_PVALB     307
Mix_4             269
Astros_1          234
Oligos_1          215
Endo              192
Mix_3             186
Mix_1             136
Ex_9_L5_6         107
Oligos_2           28
Inhib_4_SST        10
Name: count, dtype: int64
Common genes between spatial and reference: 15346

```
```python
# Step 2: Select markers in the reference
ad_sc.raw = ad_sc.copy()
sc.pp.normalize_total(ad_sc, target_sum=2000)

sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(ad_sc, flavor='seurat_v3', n_top_genes=1000)
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(ad_sc, groupby="celltype", method='wilcoxon')

# Get top 100 markers per cell type
markers_df = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.uns["rank_genes_groups"]["names"]).iloc[0:100, :]
markers = list(np.unique(markers_df.melt().value.values))

# Combine highly variable genes and markers
markers = list(set(ad_sc.var.loc[ad_sc.var['highly_variable']==True].index) | set(markers))
ad_sc.var.loc[ad_sc.var.index.isin(markers), 'Marker'] = True
ad_sc.var['Marker'] = ad_sc.var['Marker'].fillna(False)

ad_sc.var['highly_variable'] = ad_sc.var['Marker']
sel_genes = ad_sc.var.index[ad_sc.var['Marker']]

# Filter spatial data to only include marker genes
ad_spatial = ad_spatial[:, ad_spatial.var.index.isin(sel_genes)]

print(f"Selected {len(sel_genes)} marker genes for deconvolution")
print(f"Filtered spatial data: {ad_spatial.n_obs} spots × {ad_spatial.n_vars} genes")
```

```
Selected 2386 marker genes for deconvolution
Filtered spatial data: 3611 spots × 1765 genes

```
```python
# Step 3: Run RCTD deconvolution
from deconv_rctd import SpatialRNA, Reference, create_RCTD, run_RCTD

# Prepare spatial data for RCTD
counts_spatial = ad_spatial.to_df().T
coords = ad_spatial.obs[['x', 'y']]
nUMI_spatial = pd.DataFrame(np.array(ad_spatial.X.sum(-1)),
                            index=ad_spatial.obs.index)
puck = SpatialRNA(coords, counts_spatial, nUMI_spatial)

# Prepare reference data for RCTD
counts_ref = ad_sc.to_df().T
cell_types_ref = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.obs['celltype'])
nUMI_ref = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.to_df().T.sum(0))
reference = Reference(counts_ref, cell_types_ref, nUMI_ref)

print("Running RCTD deconvolution...")
# Run RCTD
myRCTD = create_RCTD(puck, reference, max_cores=20)
myRCTD = run_RCTD(myRCTD)

print("Deconvolution completed!")

# IMPORTANT: Shutdown Ray to free resources
import ray
if ray.is_initialized():
    ray.shutdown()

# IMPORTANT: Reset numba JIT state after Ray shutdown
try:
    import numba.core.registry
    numba.core.registry.cpu_target.typing_context.refresh()
    numba.core.registry.cpu_target.target_context.refresh()
except Exception:
    pass
```

```
Running RCTD deconvolution...
full
Likelihood value: 1048985.6278468096
Likelihood value: 1027272.6947050538
Likelihood value: 1009152.9043496761
Likelihood value: 1000789.8118780137
Likelihood value: 993621.6803253955
Likelihood value: 987923.0179563118
Likelihood value: 983982.0874212983
Likelihood value: 982050.0492243253
Deconvolution completed!

```
```python
# Step 4: Update session data with results
# Get filtered spots from RCTD results
filtered_spots = myRCTD["results"].index

print(f"RCTD filtered data: {len(filtered_spots)} spots retained out of {slice_0.adata.n_obs} original spots")

# Update session adata to only include filtered spots
slice_0.adata = slice_0.adata[filtered_spots, :].copy()

# Add deconvolution weights to obsm
slice_0.adata.obsm["deconv_weights"] = myRCTD["results"]
slice_0.adata.uns['has_deconv_weights'] = True

# Update the dominant celltype
dominant_celltypes = slice_0.adata.obsm["deconv_weights"].idxmax(axis=1)
slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'] = dominant_celltypes

print("Deconvolution results added to slice 0!")
print(f"\nCell type proportions available in: slice_0.adata.obsm['deconv_weights']")
print(f"Dominant cell types in: slice_0.adata.obs['celltype']")

# Show summary of results
deconv_weights = slice_0.adata.obsm["deconv_weights"]
print(f"\nDeconvolution summary:")
print(f"- Shape: {deconv_weights.shape[0]} spots × {deconv_weights.shape[1]} cell types")
print(f"- Cell types: {list(deconv_weights.columns)}")
print(f"\nMean proportions across all spots:")
print(deconv_weights.mean().round(3))

print(f"\nDominant cell type distribution:")
print(slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'].value_counts())
```

**Analysis:**
The deconvolution has successfully estimated cell type proportions for each spot. Key results to examine: (1) How many spots were retained after quality filtering, (2) The mean proportions show which cell types are most abundant overall, (3) The dominant cell type distribution shows spatial patterns, and (4) You can now access detailed proportions in `obsm['deconv_weights']` for further spatial analysis.
